# Website Code Review TODOs
Generated: Tuesday, May 19, 2026

## Home Page

- [x] [Priority: LOW] [SEO] Deprecated `<link rel="alternate" media="..." href="mobile/">` pattern on line 9. Google discourages separate mobile URLs; consider responsive design instead. (index.html:9)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Performance] Inline favicon swap script on line 12 blocks resource parsing. Consider deferring to the main script or using `defer`. (index.html:12)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Accessibility] Redundant `aria-label="OLIVER DOUGHERTY."` on `<h1>` â€” visible text already conveys the same content. Remove to avoid screen reader duplication. (index.html:66)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Performance] `resize` event listener in starfield.js is not debounced or throttled, causing repeated full canvas redraws during rapid window resizing. Wrap with `debounce` or `requestAnimationFrame`. (js/starfield.js:609)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] Debug alignment CSS classes (`.blueprint-title.debug-alignment`) left in production stylesheet. Remove or guard behind a build flag. (css/schematic.css:652-666)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] NOTE: Duplicate `background` property in light-mode `.card` rules is an intentional fallback pattern: line 619 sets `background: #F7F8F6` as a fallback for browsers that don't support `color-mix()` on line 621. Same pattern used throughout the flair system. (css/design-system.css:619-621)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] NOTE: `!important` on `.accent-hover:hover` color in schematic.css is intentional â€” `.accent-hover` is a utility class applied across multiple pages/themes, and `!important` ensures the accent color overrides theme-specific overrides. (css/schematic.css:147)

## Resume Page

- [x] [Priority: MED] [Accessibility] Navigation overlay uses a `<div>` instead of a `<nav>` element. Screen readers rely on `<nav>` landmarks to identify navigation regions. Wrap `#navOverlay` content in `<nav>` or add `role="navigation"`. (pages/resume/index.html:40)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Code Quality] Duplicate `.resume-hero` CSS rules both set `border-bottom: 1px solid #000000` and `background: #FFFFFF`. The generic `.resume-hero` rule (line 168-172) is redundant with the more specific `.resume-hero.schematic-wrapper` rule (line 125-133). Remove the duplicate declarations. (css/resume.css:168-172)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Accessibility] Decorative SVG icons inside `.contact-item` links are missing `aria-hidden="true"`. While the links have text labels, marking decorative SVGs as hidden prevents screen readers from announcing SVG element types. (pages/resume/index.html:77, 84)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Performance] NOTE: Google Fonts stylesheet on line 26 already includes `&display=swap` parameter. No action needed. (pages/resume/index.html:26)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] Large inline typing animation script (~130 lines) blocks initial HTML parsing and cannot be cached separately. Extract to an external file like `js/resume-typing.js` with `defer`. (pages/resume/index.html:252-384)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Error Handling] No null check before `cursor.offsetHeight` (line 337). If `#typeCursor` is missing from the DOM, `getElementById` returns null and `.offsetHeight` throws a TypeError. Add a guard. (pages/resume/index.html:337)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] NOTE: `!important` on `.nav-overlay-bg { background-color: #FFFFFF !important; }` is intentional â€” it overrides the dark-mode schematic styles on the resume page which has a light-only color scheme. The `!important` is needed because the schematic CSS uses equally specific selectors. (css/resume.css:71)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Performance] Inline favicon swap `<script>` on line 21 is not deferred and blocks resource parsing. Move to a deferred script or use the `defer` attribute. (pages/resume/index.html:21)

## Photo Gallery Page
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Accessibility] NOTE: Hero feature `<img>` tag starts with empty `alt=""` but the alt text is populated dynamically via JS in `syncHeroFeature()` (line 314 of gallery.js). The button wrapping the image has `aria-label="Open featured photo"` which provides context. Per the rules, images with alt="" dynamically populated by JS are NOT missing alt. (pages/gallery/index.html:70)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Accessibility] NOTE: Lightbox `<img>` tag starts with empty `alt=""` but alt text is set dynamically in `renderLightboxEntry()` (line 623 of gallery.js). Per the rules, images with alt="" dynamically populated by JS are NOT missing alt. (pages/gallery/index.html:162)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Accessibility] Lightbox has `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"` but no `inert` attribute is applied to the rest of the page when the lightbox opens. Non-interactive elements outside the lightbox remain focusable unless the focus trap catches them. Consider adding `inert` to `<main>` when lightbox opens. (js/gallery.js:597)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Performance] Google Fonts stylesheet loaded without `&display=swap` parameter. Default `display=auto` can block text rendering for up to 3 seconds while fonts load. Append `&display=swap` to the font URL. (pages/gallery/index.html:15)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Performance] `fetch` requests use `cache: 'no-cache'` for both manifest and sequence JSON files. These data files rarely change and could benefit from standard HTTP caching (omit the option or use `cache: 'default'`) to avoid revalidating on every page load. (js/gallery.js:167)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `buildNarrativeCopy()` function always returns an empty string `''`. This is dead code -- the function is called on line 634 but contributes nothing. Either implement it or remove the call and the function. (js/gallery.js:648-650)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `formatTitle()` applies the same file extension regex replacement twice in sequence (lines 938-939). The second `.replace()` is redundant since the first already stripped the extension. Remove the duplicate call. (js/gallery.js:938-939)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `.hero-subtext` CSS class is set to `display: none` with a comment "Removed from HTML, keeping class here for safety if added back". Dead CSS that should be removed if the element is truly gone. (css/gallery.css:191-192)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] NOTE: `!important` on `.hero-feature-media.schematic-border { border: 1px solid #000000 !important; }` overrides the default rounded border in gallery.css â€” intentional schematic mode override. `!important` on `.photo-card { --reveal-delay: 0ms !important; }` in mobile breakpoint is intentional to disable stagger animation on mobile. Both are utility-class-style overrides. (css/gallery.css:139, 985)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Performance] Inline favicon swap `<script>` on line 11 blocks resource parsing. Move to a deferred script or use the `defer` attribute. (pages/gallery/index.html:11)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Accessibility] `lightbox-meta-summary` uses `list-style: none` to hide the native details/summary marker, but the `<summary>` element has no `aria-label` or visual indicator for screen readers that it's expandable. The `::after` content '+' is visual-only. (css/gallery.css:755)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Error Handling] `renderLightboxEntry()` accesses `gallery.elements.lightboxImage.style` and multiple `gallery.elements.*` properties without null checks. If the lightbox DOM is missing, this throws a TypeError. (js/gallery.js:620-645)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `navigateLightbox()` sets inline `style.opacity` on the lightbox image for fade transitions. This inline style persists after the transition and is never cleaned up, which can cause unexpected behavior if the image element is reused. (js/gallery.js:720-732)

## Utilities Home Page

- [x] [Priority: MED] [Browser Compatibility] `color-mix()` used extensively throughout the flair color system (15+ declarations). Firefox does not support `color-mix()` as of mid-2024. The current fallback pattern (hardcoded duplicate declarations before the `color-mix` line) is fragile and hard to maintain â€” a single missed fallback leaves a broken variable. Consider a PostCSS fallback plugin, `@supports (color: color-mix(in srgb, red, blue))` guards, or a build-time fallback generator. (css/utilities.css:1849-1968)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Code Quality] NOTE: 15+ duplicate CSS property declarations throughout the flair section (e.g., `--flair-dark` on lines 1849 and 1852, `box-shadow` on lines 1865-1866, `background` on lines 1879-1880, etc.) are intentional fallbacks for `color-mix()` in Firefox. While the pattern is acknowledged as fragile (see item above), these are not accidental duplicates. (css/utilities.css:1849-1968)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Accessibility] `.vm-status-dot` is a purely visual color-coded status indicator with no accessible text equivalent. Screen readers won't convey VM state changes (idle, loading, running, fullscreen, resetting, error, unsupported). Add `aria-hidden="true"` to the dot element and ensure all state changes are announced through the existing `aria-live="polite"` region (`#retroVmStatusText`). (pages/utilities/index.html:284)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Code Quality] VM `<section>` element has 18+ data attributes crammed onto a single tag, making it extremely hard to read, review, and maintain. Extract to a `<script type="application/json" id="vm-config">` block or a dedicated data element. (pages/utilities/index.html:278)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Accessibility] `#deathStatusText` uses deprecated `clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0)` for screen-reader-only text. Replace with modern `clip-path: inset(50%)` or the standard `.sr-only` utility class. (css/utilities.css:2015)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Performance] Inline IIFE script for title card button reveal animation (~80 lines) cannot be cached across page loads and runs before deferred scripts. Extract to `js/utilities-title-reveal.js` with `defer`. (pages/utilities/index.html:380-458)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Accessibility] Back button click handler calls `setHash(null)` which triggers `showTitleCard()`, but focus is not programmatically moved. After returning to the title card, focus remains on the back button (which is now hidden). Move focus to the title heading or first utility button after transition completes. (js/utilities-shell.js:280-282)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `loadLocalAssistantScript()` caches its promise in a module-level variable. If the script fails to load (line 118-121), the promise is reset to `null`, but the dynamically inserted `<script>` element is only removed from the DOM â€” if the script partially loaded and initialized globals, a second load attempt may cause duplicate initialization. Consider a load-attempt counter or cleanup hook. (js/utilities-shell.js:104-129)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `opts = opts ?? {}` uses the nullish coalescing operator (`??`), which is ES2020. While widely supported, if the project targets older browsers, prefer `opts = opts || {}` for broader compatibility. (js/utilities-shell.js:192)

## Fourier Audio Utility

- [x] [Priority: MED] [Error Handling] `catch (_error)` in `createAudioWaveRenderer()` silently swallows WebGL initialization errors when falling back to Canvas2D. The error is not logged anywhere, making it impossible to debug why WebGL failed in production. Log the error at minimum. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierWaveRenderer.ts:620)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Error Handling] `AudioContext.resume()` error on line 573-576 is only logged via `logAudioFourierWarning` which is gated to localhost. On production, a blocked AudioContext resume is silently swallowed with no user-visible feedback. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierController.ts:573-576)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [TypeScript] Non-null assertion `this.activeResult!` on line 1019. While guarded by the null check at line 997, the assertion masks a potential race condition if `stopPlayback` is called between the check and usage. Use a local variable capture instead. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierController.ts:1019)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Code Quality] `this.durationLabel.textContent` is set to `${result.metadata.timingsMs.total.toFixed(0)} ms` (line 772), which shows computation time in milliseconds, not audio duration. The element is named "Duration" which implies audio length. This is misleading UX. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierController.ts:772)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Code Quality] `void this.generate()` on line 224 is a fire-and-forget async call. If `generate()` throws an unhandled rejection (e.g., from `resolveAudioSource`), it becomes an unhandled promise rejection. Add `.catch()` or restructure. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierController.ts:224)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [TypeScript] Unsafe type assertion `self as unknown as DedicatedWorkerGlobalScope` uses double-cast through `unknown`. (utilities-src/src/audioFourier.worker.ts:15)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [TypeScript] Unsafe type assertion `as WebGLRenderingContext | null` on lines 285 and 631. The `getContext` chain can return `WebGL2RenderingContext` which is a subtype but the assertion discards the distinction. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierWaveRenderer.ts:285, 631)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [TypeScript] Non-null assertions `getContext('2d')!` on lines 348 and 380 bypass type safety on offscreen canvases. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierController.ts:348, 380)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [TypeScript] Unsafe type assertion `button.dataset.audioPreset as BuiltInAudioPresetId` on line 248. Guarded by the `in` check on line 249, but the assertion itself is not type-safe. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierController.ts:248)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Error Handling] `catch (_error)` on line 1077 silently swallows `source.stop()` errors. The comment explains why, but a debug-mode log would help with edge-case diagnosis. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierController.ts:1077)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Browser Compatibility] `Object.hasOwn()` used on line 88 is ES2022. If the project targets older browsers, prefer `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call()`. (utilities-src/src/audioPresets.ts:88)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Browser Compatibility] `Worker` with `{ type: 'module' }` option on line 672 requires ES Module Worker support. Not supported in Safari < 14.1 or older browsers. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierController.ts:672)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Performance] `buildEnergyBandReconstruction()` allocates ~336 MB for `bandSamples` (12 bands x 7M samples x 4 bytes) as noted in the code comment. This is a significant memory spike that could OOM on lower-end devices. Consider warning users or providing a lower-band-count option. (utilities-src/src/audioFourierCore.ts:571-573)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [TypeScript] `resolveWorkerRequestId()` on lines 35-41 accesses `(request as { requestId?: unknown }).requestId` via type assertion. This is a duck-typing pattern that bypasses TypeScript safety. (utilities-src/src/audioFourier.worker.ts:35-41)

## Local Assistant Utility

- [x] [Priority: HIGH] [Code Quality] The entire safe-rendering pipeline (LocalLlmLatexParser, splitInlineLocalLlmSegments, renderInlineLocalLlmText, renderMarkdownInlineText, renderSafeText, renderLocalLlmMath, escapeHtml, LATEX_COMMANDS, cleanupModelText) is fully duplicated between `js/local-llm-chat.js` (lines 1175-1512) and `utilities-src/src/localLlmState.ts` (lines 39-365). Two independent implementations of the same logic will diverge over time. Extract to a shared module or have one import from the other.
- [x] [Priority: HIGH] [Code Quality] `cleanupModelText` is triplicated across `js/local-llm-chat.js:1175`, `js/local-llm-worker.js:449`, and `utilities-src/src/localLlmState.ts:39`. All three contain identical regex chains for stripping think tags and chat-template markers. Consolidate into a single shared utility.
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Performance] `renderMessages()` (line 819) rebuilds the entire message list via `replaceChildren()` on every call. During streaming, `updateAssistantElement()` calls `renderSafeText()` (a full markdown + LaTeX parse) on every token batch and sets `innerHTML`. Consider incremental DOM patches or a lightweight text-append for the streaming draft element.
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Browser Compatibility] `color-mix()` used extensively in `css/local-llm-chat.css` (20+ declarations across lines 56-734) without any `@supports` guard or fallback for Firefox, which does not support `color-mix()`.
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `escapeHtml` duplicated in `js/local-llm-chat.js:1505` and `utilities-src/src/localLlmState.ts:358`. `LATEX_COMMANDS` duplicated at `js/local-llm-chat.js:1223` and `utilities-src/src/localLlmState.ts:93`. `LocalLlmLatexParser` class duplicated at `js/local-llm-chat.js:1341` and `utilities-src/src/localLlmState.ts:211`.
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Error Handling] Empty catch block on `js/local-llm-chat.js:1034` swallows errors from `worker.postMessage` during dispose teardown. A `console.debug` log would aid diagnosis.
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Error Handling] Empty catch block on `js/local-llm-worker.js:258` swallows errors from `generator.dispose()` with only a comment. Add a debug log.
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Error Handling] Fire-and-forget `void this.clearBrowserModelCaches()` on `js/local-llm-chat.js:984` and `:1005` -- unhandled rejections from cache deletion are silently dropped.
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Accessibility] Deprecated `clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0)` used for visually-hidden elements in `css/local-llm-chat.css:156` and `:688`. Replace with `clip-path: inset(50%)`.
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] Blank lines 522-523 in `css/local-llm-chat.css` appear to be leftover from editing. Remove.

## Virtual Machine Utility

- [x] [Priority: MED] [Code Quality] Redundant nested try/catch in `requestPointerLock()`. The inner try/catch on lines 298-302 catches errors from the `{ unadjustedMovement: true }` call and immediately re-throws to the outer try/catch on lines 295-306, which catches and logs the same error. The inner catch adds no value -- remove it so the outer handler covers both paths. (utilities-src/src/retroVmController.ts:295-306)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [TypeScript] `cdromSizeBytes` is typed as `number | null` but the ternary on line 140 uses truthiness check (`config.cdromSizeBytes ? ...`). If `cdromSizeBytes` were ever `0`, the `size` property would be omitted even though `0` is a valid number. Use `config.cdromSizeBytes != null` instead. (utilities-src/src/retroVmConfig.ts:140)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Error Handling] Fire-and-forget `void this.destroySession()` in `dispose()` on line 909 silently drops rejections. If `destroySession()` rejects (e.g., `emulator.destroy()` fails), the error is lost with no logging. Add `.catch()` with a debug log. NOTE: `beforeUnloadHandler` on line 361 already has `.catch()` that logs via `debugRetroVm()`. Only line 909 needs fixing. (utilities-src/src/retroVmController.ts:909, 361)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Error Handling] `navigator.maxTouchPoints` is accessed on line 51 without checking whether `navigator` is defined. The `hasWindow` guard only checks for `window`, not `navigator`. In restricted environments, `navigator` may be absent. (utilities-src/src/retroVmSupport.ts:51)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `launchButton.disabled` on line 880 chains 6 conditions in a single expression. Extract to a named helper or computed property for readability and testability. (utilities-src/src/retroVmController.ts:880)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [TypeScript] Unsafe double-cast `as unknown as (query: string) => MediaQueryList` in test mocks. Use a typed factory or `jest.fn()`/`vi.fn()` instead of runtime type assertions. (utilities-src/tests/retroVmSupport.test.ts:10-12, 32, 64, 72)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `buildRetroVmV86Options()` passes all optional network config fields (`routerMac`, `routerIp`, `vmIp`, etc.) to `net_device` even when they are `undefined`. While v86 likely ignores undefined fields, it adds noise to the options object. Only include defined fields. (utilities-src/src/retroVmConfig.ts:155-162)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] Test uses `as Partial<Record<string, string>>` type assertion on line 43 to bypass TypeScript's excess property check, masking the fact that `readRetroVmDatasetConfig` silently drops unknown keys. Consider adding an explicit test verifying unknown keys are absent from the output. (utilities-src/tests/retroVmConfig.test.ts:43-44)

## Stress Test Utility

- [x] [Priority: MED] [Error Handling] Fire-and-forget `void this.start()` on line 273 is an unhandled async call. If `start()` rejects before the internal try/catch (e.g., from a synchronous exception during state setup), the rejection is silently dropped with no user feedback. Wrap with `.catch()` or use an async IIFE with error handling. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:273)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Code Quality] `lastGpuError` field is set for CPU worker errors (lines 506, 510) despite the name implying GPU-only. Rename to `lastError` or split into `lastCpuError` / `lastGpuError` for clarity. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:506, 510)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Code Quality] `stopGpuStress()` calls `loseContext()` on line 1005 during normal cleanup. If the WebGL context is shared with other page elements (e.g., other utilities), this destroys the shared context. Only call `loseContext()` during error paths, not during graceful stop. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:1005)
- [x] [Priority: MED] [Accessibility] NOTE: The canvas element in the HTML template at line 362 already includes `aria-label="Stress test GPU output"`. The `replaceCanvasElement()` method copies this attribute to the replacement canvas. No action needed. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:1368, pages/utilities/index.html:362)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] Trailing blank line inside the `for` loop body at line 1143 (after closing brace of the lane loop, before `ctx.restore()`). Minor style inconsistency. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:1143)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Browser Compatibility] `Worker` with `{ type: 'module' }` on line 439 requires ES Module Worker support. Not supported in Safari < 14.1 or older browsers. The CPU stress path has no fallback for browsers lacking module worker support. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:439)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Browser Compatibility] `performance.now()` used extensively (lines 352, 755, 944, 1188, 1218, etc.) -- ES2016 API. While widely supported, older browsers may lack it. Consider a `Date.now()` fallback. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:352)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [TypeScript] Multiple type assertions on `globalThis` for WebGPU constants (lines 166, 175) bypass type safety. While WebGPU types aren't in the standard lib yet, consider importing from `@types/dom-webgpu` when available. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:166, 175)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] The built JS bundle at `pages/utilities/assets/stressTestController.js` includes a source map reference (`//# sourceMappingURL=stressTestController.js.map` on line 122). Source maps in production expose the full source code and internal implementation details. Remove the sourceMappingURL comment from the production build. (pages/utilities/assets/stressTestController.js:122)
- [x] [Priority: LOW] [Code Quality] `handleWorkerMessage()` on lines 500-514 stops both CPU and GPU stress on unexpected worker messages. This aggressive error handling is reasonable for a stress test but could be softened to log and ignore unknown message types instead of halting the entire test. (utilities-src/src/stressTestController.ts:500-514)
